name: Barn Golf Club (Ogden)
address: 305 W Pleasant View Dr, Ogden, UT 84414, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday
editorial_note: Located just 2 miles East of I-15 at exit 349 is your #1 option for golf in Ogden Utah. The Barn Golf Club is a privately owned and family-operated golf course located in the foothills of the majestic Ben Lomond peak. Featuring 18 beautiful golf holes along with 2 practice greens, a driving range, a full-service snack bar, and a well-stocked Pro shop full of top brand name merchandise.

name: Bear Lake (Garden City)
address: 222 Clubhouse Dr, Garden City, UT 84028, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday
editorial_note: Come out and experience Bear Lake Golf Course. Bear Lake is an exciting 9 hole regulation course located at the Bear Lake Golf Course facility in Garden City, UT. From the longest tees it presents 3,376 yards of golf for a par of 36. The course was designed by William H. Neff, ASGCA and opened in 1971.

name: Black Desert Resort (Ivins)
address: 1500 E Black Desert Dr, Ivins, UT 84738, USA
rate_notes:
2026 Rates        January 2 - March 12        March 13 – July 6        July 7 – September 3, 10:15 AM - Close        September 4 – January 1, 2027
Resort Guest:        $250        $325        $250        $325
Non-Resort Guest:        $325        $450        $325        $450
editorial_note: Vermilion cliffs. Cobalt skies. Crimson peaks. In a landscape this breathtaking, we offer something more—Tom Weiskopf’s final masterpiece. Discover why Golfweek named Black Desert Resort the #1 course in Utah. You’ll find it on many of the prestigious publication’s most coveted lists: #34 on their nationwide “Top 100 Best Courses you can play in the U.S.” ranking, and #96 on the “Top 200 Modern Courses” list.

name: Bonneville (SLC)
address: 954 Connor St, Salt Lake City, UT 84108, USA
rate_notes: 18-hole rate; 9 holes $25 (off 10th tee first 90 min daily). 18 holes only until 4pm Fri-Sun and holidays. Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $20/player.
editorial_note: Now, almost one hundred years since the opening of the original nine-holes, Bonneville continues to be renowned for its superb conditions and classic layout. Situated on the East Bench of Salt Lake City, just 5 minutes away from the heart of downtown, this gem offers awe-inspiring sunsets and views of both the surrounding mountains and the entire Salt Lake valley.

name: Bountiful Ridge (Bountiful)
address: 2430 Bountiful Blvd, Bountiful, UT 84010, USA
rate_notes: 18-hole rates: Mon-Thu $36, Fri-Sun & holidays $46; 9 holes $18/$23. All tee times prepaid; max 2 tee times per person per day. Season roughly Mar-Nov.
editorial_note: Perched on the east bench with panoramic lake and valley views. Legit elevation change — one of the tougher walks on the Wasatch Front. No driving range, so warm up before you arrive; the short 4-day window means weekend times vanish fast.
history_blurb: Opened in 1975; designed by William H. Neff.

name: Canyon Breeze (Beaver)
address: 371 UT-153, Beaver, UT 84713, USA
rate_notes: Same Rates Monday - Sunday
editorial_note: Canyon Breeze Golf Course is designed to serve the residents of Beaver while welcoming golfers from across Utah, by providing a well-maintained, challenging, enjoyable, and affordable golf experience.

name: Canyon Hills (Nephi)
address: 1200 E 100 N, Nephi, UT 84648, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday

name: Carbon CC (Helper)
address: 3055 US-6, Helper, UT 84526, USA
rate_notes: Same Rates Monday - Sunday
editorial_note: Located midway between Helper and Price, Carbon Country Club is where you will discover the alluring beauty of the Utah frontier sculpted into a one-of-a-kind golf experience. Set within chiseled mesas and rocky valleys, play our golf course that continues to be an oasis for our members and guests year after year. Whether new to the game or a passionate veteran of golf, Carbon Country Club offers you the experience that you will forever enjoy.

name: Cedar Hills (Cedar Hills)
address: 10640 Clubhouse Dr, Cedar Hills, UT 84062, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday
Carts Mandatory on Weekends/Holidays

name: Cedar Ridge (Cedar City)
address: 200 E 900 N, Cedar City, UT 84721, USA
rate_notes: Same Rates Monday - Sunday
editorial_note: Cedar Ridge is a fun 18-hole golf course located against the red hills on the east bench of Cedar City. Cedar Ridge is a par 72 that offers plenty of eagle and birdie opportunities. The course has 3 sets of tees for golfers of all different playing abilities.

name: Cove View (Richfield)
address: 1315 W 1800 S, Richfield, UT 84701, USA
rate_notes: Same Rates Monday - Sunday
editorial_note: Cove View has become a much sought-after golf course in Central Utah. It is a popular destination for many scratch golfers as well as travelers on I-70.

name: Crane Field (Clinton)
address: 3648 Cranefield Rd, Clinton, UT 84015, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday

name: Davis Park (Kaysville)
address: 1074 Nicholls Rd, Kaysville, UT 84037, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday
editorial_note: Davis Park Golf Course is a public golf course located on the benches of Fruit Heights, Utah. The course has beautiful views of the valley, lake, and mountains. It is known for its smooth putting surfaces and great staff.

name: Dinaland (Vernal)
address: 675 S 2000 E, Vernal, UT 84078, USA
rate_notes:
Weekday Rates: Monday - Thursday
Weekend Rates: Friday - Sunday
editorial_note: With the breath taking views of Split Mountain and a challenging 18-hole course, Dinaland Golf Course is a favorite of locals and visitors! This course is one golfers of all ages and experience levels will love to golf over and over again.

name: Dixie Red Hills (St. George)
address: 645 W 1250 N, St. George, UT 84770, USA
rate_notes:
Season Prices: 
October-May
June-September (Cheaper)
editorial_note: Dixie Red Hills Golf Course was the first golf course developed by the City of St. George in the mid 1960’s taking us from a desert gas station stop to a true place of beauty and tourism.

name: Eagle Mountain (Brigham City)
address: 960 E 700 S, Brigham City, UT 84302, USA
editorial_note: Brigham City-area course with valley and mountain views — a straightforward northern-Utah round.

name: Eaglewood (N. Salt Lake)
address: 1110 E Eaglewood Dr, North Salt Lake, UT 84054, USA

name: El Monte (Ogden)
address: 1300 Valley Dr, Ogden, UT 84401, USA
editorial_note: A historic little 9 at the mouth of Ogden Canyon — short, shady, and cheap, with canyon walls right there. One of the oldest courses in the state and a quick, charming loop.
history_blurb: One of Utah’s oldest courses, dating to the 1920s at the mouth of Ogden Canyon.

name: Forest Dale (SLC)
address: 2375 S 900 E, Salt Lake City, UT 84106, USA
rate_notes: 9-hole course; 18-hole cells reflect two loops. Same rate all week. Indoor simulators on site. Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $10/player. Cart per rider.
editorial_note: Tight, tree-lined 9 in Sugar House with old-school charm and a walkable footprint. Great winter option with the simulator building.
history_blurb: Opened in the early 1900s as the original home of the Salt Lake Country Club — one of the oldest courses in Utah.

name: Fore Lakes (Taylorsville)
address: 1285 W 4700 S, Taylorsville, UT 84123, USA
editorial_note: Executive/par-3 course in Taylorsville — short, flat, and easy; a good spot for beginners or a fast practice round.

name: Fox Hollow (American Fork)
address: 1400 N 200 E, American Fork, UT 84003, USA
editorial_note: Popular American Fork course with water and mature trees — playable for most handicaps and a reliable Utah County option.

name: Gladstan (Payson)
address: 1 Gladstan Dr, Payson, UT 84651, USA
editorial_note: A hilly, tight mountain-valley course above Payson with big views — consistently one of the best-value 18s in the state. Positioning matters; it punishes wayward tee shots.

name: Glen Eagle (Syracuse)
address: 3176 W 1700 S, Syracuse, UT 84075, USA
editorial_note: Flat, open Davis County course in Syracuse — roomy and walkable, an unpretentious everyday track.

name: Glendale (SLC)
address: 1630 W 2100 S, Salt Lake City, UT 84119, USA
rate_notes: Same public rate weekdays & weekends; passes not valid before 2pm on weekends/holidays. Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $15/player. Cart priced per rider.
editorial_note: West-side muni along the Jordan River with wide, forgiving fairways and some of the most in-demand tee times in the valley. Flat, fast walk. Be online the minute the 7-day window opens.

name: Green Spring (St. George)
address: 588 North Green Spring Drive, Washington, UT 84780, USA
editorial_note: Best known for its lava-rock quarry stretch — the mid-round holes plunging through a black-rock ravine are among the most photographed in southern Utah. Semi-private feel, upper-tier conditioning.
signature_hole: The quarry holes (8-10) — forced carries across a lava-rock ravine

name: Hobble Creek (Springville)
address: 94 Hobble Creek Canyon Rd, Springville, UT 84663, USA
editorial_note: Tucked up Hobble Creek Canyon — tree-lined, scenic, and a local favorite, especially in fall color. Some uphill stretches but a rewarding walk.

name: Homestead (Midway)
address: 700 Homestead Dr, Midway, UT 84049, USA
editorial_note: Resort course in the Heber Valley next to the Homestead Crater — mature, well-conditioned, and set against the mountains. A relaxed destination round with a resort feel.

name: Lakeside (West Bountiful)
address: 1201 N 1100 W, West Bountiful, UT 84087, USA
editorial_note: Flat, water-dotted course in West Bountiful — easy walking and forgiving off the tee. A good pick for a laid-back round or newer players.

name: Logan River (Logan)
address: 550 W 1000 S, Logan, UT 84321, USA
editorial_note: Flat course winding along the Logan River in Cache Valley — walkable and scenic, with water frequently in play. Cooler northern-Utah climate.

name: Meadow Brook (SLC)
address: 4197 S 1300 W, Taylorsville, UT 84123, USA
editorial_note: Classic old-style parkland in the center of the valley — flat, easy walk, forgiving, and cheap for a regulation 18. A solid choice when the marquee courses are booked out.

name: Mick Riley (Murray)
address: 421 E Vine St, Murray, UT 84107, USA
editorial_note: The valley’s beginner headquarters — a friendly regulation 9 plus a separate par-3 course, full range, and the county’s junior golf hub. Flat, cheap, and zero intimidation factor.

name: Mountain Dell (SLC)
address: Parleys Canyon, I-80, Exit #134, 7603 E Mountain Dell Golf Rd, Salt Lake City, UT 84109, USA
rate_notes: Two 18s (Lake & Canyon), same rate. 18 holes only until 4pm Thu-Sun/holidays. Weekend/holiday 9-hole cart is $20/rider (vs $10 weekday). Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $20/player.
editorial_note: Two 18s up Parleys Canyon — genuine mountain golf 15 minutes from downtown. Cooler temps, elevation change, and the occasional elk. Canyon is the sportier walk; Lake plays bigger.

name: Mountain View (West Jordan)
address: 2400 Gardner Ln, West Jordan, UT 84088, USA
editorial_note: Spacious fairways, generous greens, and wall-to-wall Wasatch views from the west side. Straightforward, flat walk that suits every skill level — a dependable mid-priced 18.

name: Mt. Ogden (Ogden)
address: 1787 Constitution Way, Ogden, UT 84403, USA
editorial_note: Foothill course beneath the Ogden peaks — hilly, scenic, and a workout to walk, with mountain views on nearly every hole.

name: Murray Parkway (Murray)
address: 6345 Murray Pkwy Ave, Murray, UT 84123, USA
editorial_note: Flat, mature parkway course along the Jordan River in Murray — central, walkable, and consistently good value. An easy after-work 18.

name: Nibley Park (SLC)
address: 2780 S 700 E, Salt Lake City, UT 84106, USA
rate_notes: 9-hole course; 18-hole cells reflect two loops. Same rate all week. Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $10/player. Cart per rider.
editorial_note: Historic city 9-holer — short, social, and beginner-friendly. The classic grab-a-quick-nine course in the middle of town.
history_blurb: Dates to the early 1920s on land donated by Charles W. Nibley — one of Salt Lake City's oldest public courses.

name: Old Mill (SLC)
address: 6080 Wasatch Blvd, Holladay, UT 84121, USA
editorial_note: The premium county track — bench-side setting off I-215 with big valley views, a legit clubhouse, and conditions a cut above the other county courses. Priced accordingly at $44.
history_blurb: Gene Bates design; one of the newer courses in the Salt Lake area.

name: Oquirrh Hills (Tooele)
address: 1255 E Vine St, Tooele, UT 84074, USA
editorial_note: Tooele Valley muni at the base of the Oquirrh Mountains — rolling terrain, mountain views, and honest small-town value.

name: Park City Golf Club (Park City)
address: 1541 Thaynes Canyon Dr, Park City, UT 84060, USA
editorial_note: The in-town Park City muni, right off Main Street with ski-run views — mountain setting, walkable core, and cooler summer temps than the valley. Books up in peak season.

name: Purple Sage Golf Course (WY)
address: 401 Kindler Dr, Evanston, WY 82930, USA
editorial_note: A 9-holer just over the line in Evanston, WY — small-town value and a quick loop; still on Mountain time.

name: Remuda (Farr West)
address: 2600 W 3500 N, Farr West, UT 84404, USA
editorial_note: Open, flattish course out in Farr West with a driving range — roomy and walkable, an unpretentious everyday track north of Ogden.

name: River Oaks (Sandy)
address: 9300 Riverside Dr, Sandy, UT 84070, USA
editorial_note: Sandy municipal course along the Jordan River — flat, walkable, water in play, and dependable everyday value.

name: Riverbend (Riverton)
address: 1040 W 12800 S, Riverton, UT 84065, USA
editorial_note: Winds along the Jordan River at the south end of the valley with elevated tees, wetlands, and a country feel. Full practice area and one of the biggest men’s leagues around (400+), so Wednesdays are busy.
history_blurb: Gene Bates design along the Jordan River.

name: Roosevelt (Roosevelt)
address: 1155 Club House Dr, Roosevelt, UT 84066, USA

name: Rose Park (SLC)
address: 1386 N Redwood Rd, Salt Lake City, UT 84116, USA
rate_notes: Same public rate weekdays & weekends; passes not valid before 2pm on weekends/holidays. Advance reservation outside 7-day window: $15/player. Cart priced per rider.
editorial_note: Flat, walkable, and quick by SLC standards — a go-to for after-work nines. Wind is the main defense. One of the best value 18s in the city.

name: Sand Hollow Championship (Hurricane)
address: 5662 West Clubhouse Drive, Hurricane, UT 84737, USA
editorial_note: The most nationally recognized public course in Utah — links-inspired golf through red sandstone with the famous cliffside stretch above the reservoir. Books out early; play spring or fall, not July heat.
signature_hole: No. 15 — the clifftop par 3 over the sandstone canyon

name: Sand Hollow Links (Hurricane)
address: 5662 West Clubhouse Drive, Hurricane, UT 84737, USA
editorial_note: The 9-hole links companion to the Championship course — a genuine British-style layout with fescue and pot bunkers. Underrated and far easier to get on than its big sibling.

name: Sky Mountain (Hurricane)
address: 1030 N 2600 W, Hurricane, UT 84737, USA
editorial_note: One of the best scenery-per-dollar rounds in the state — a well-priced desert-mountain course with jaw-dropping views toward Zion. Elevation changes make it a real walk.

name: Sleepy Ridge (Orem)
address: 730 S Sleepy Ridge Dr, Orem, UT 84058, USA
editorial_note: Links-style layout near Utah Lake with wetlands and water in play — flatter and walkable, with mountain views across the valley. Popular for events.

name: South Mountain (Draper)
address: 1247 E Mike Weir Dr, Draper, UT 84020, USA
editorial_note: Mountain-links style on the Draper foothills — dramatic elevation change, huge undulating greens, five tee sets, and a range that opens May through October. Note for walkers: carts are mandatory here, so this one never counts toward the on-foot list.
history_blurb: Opened in 1998, designed by David Graham and Gary Panks. Host of the 1999 Utah Open and once ranked as high as #3 in Utah by Golf Digest.

name: Southgate (St. George)
address: 1975 S Tonaquint Dr, St. George, UT 84770, USA
editorial_note: Flat, mature, forgiving city course along the Santa Clara River — an easy walk and a friendly choice for higher handicaps or a relaxed round.

name: St. George Golf Club (St. George)
address: 2190 S 1400 E, St. George, UT 84790, USA
editorial_note: Longtime city course on the east side — solid, roomy, and dependable, with mature trees and good value. A local workhorse rather than a destination card.

name: Stonebridge (West Valley City)
address: 4415 Links Dr, West Valley City, UT 84120, USA
editorial_note: A 27-hole Johnny Miller links design in West Valley — flat, water-laced, and playable, with three nines to mix and match. One of the more forgiving valley walks.

name: Sun Hills (Layton)
address: 3185 N N Hls Dr, Layton, UT 84041, USA
editorial_note: Foothill course on the Layton bench — some elevation and good mountain views, a dependable local option.

name: Sunbrook (St. George)
address: 2366 W Sunbrook Dr, St. George, UT 84770, USA
editorial_note: St. George’s well-regarded 27-hole city facility (Woodbridge, Pointe, Blackrock nines). Blackrock plays through lava rock along the Santa Clara River. Great muni value.

name: SunRiver Golf Club (St. George)
address: 4210 Bluegrass Way, St. George, UT 84790, USA
editorial_note: Flat, immaculately kept course inside the SunRiver active-adult community — wide, walkable, and mellow. Popular with snowbirds and easy on the legs.

name: TalonsCove (Saratoga Springs)
address: 2220 S TalonsCove Dr, Saratoga Springs, UT 84045, USA
editorial_note: Saratoga Springs course with open, rolling terrain and lake-valley views — a newer west-side option for Utah County.

name: Thanksgiving Point (Lehi)
address: 3300 W Club House Dr, Lehi, UT 84043, USA
editorial_note: A Johnny Miller design and one of the longest, most demanding public courses in the state — water, elevation, and length everywhere. From the tips it’s a beast; move up a tee or two.
history_blurb: Johnny Miller design; among the longest courses in Utah.

name: The Ledges (St. George)
address: 1585 Ledges Pkwy, St. George, UT 84770, USA
editorial_note: Upscale layout on the north bench with dramatic elevation and red-rock backdrops toward Snow Canyon. Higher-end conditioning and prices to match.

name: The Oaks at Spanish Fork (Spanish Fork)
address: 2300 E Powerhouse Rd, Spanish Fork, UT 84660, USA
editorial_note: Riverside city course in Spanish Fork — flat, walkable, and affordable, with the Spanish Fork River threading through. A solid everyday muni.

name: The Ranches (Eagle Mtn)
address: Clubhouse Ln, Eagle Mountain, UT 84005, USA
editorial_note: Exposed links-style course out in Eagle Mountain — windswept and open, with few trees to stop the breeze. Plays firm and fast; wind is the whole defense.

name: The Ridge (West Valley)
address: 5055 Westridge Blvd, West Valley City, UT 84118, USA
editorial_note: A Gene Bates design in the Oquirrh foothills with sweeping Wasatch and downtown views — among the more sought-after public tee times on the west side.

name: Timpanogos Championship (Provo)
address: 380 E 1860 S, Provo, UT 84606, USA
editorial_note: The championship 18 at the East Bay complex in Provo — roomy, water in play, and a steady Utah County muni for a full round.

name: Timpanogos Pasture (Provo)
address: 380 E 1860 S, Provo, UT 84606, USA
editorial_note: The shorter Pasture course at East Bay in Provo — a quick, flat, low-key 9 for practice or a fast loop.

name: Valley View (Layton)
address: 2501 E Gentile St, Layton, UT 84040, USA
editorial_note: Davis County bench course in Layton — rolling terrain, valley views, and steady muni value.

name: Wolf Creek (Eden)
address: 3718 N Wolf Creek Dr, Eden, UT 84310, USA
editorial_note: Dramatic mountain golf in the Ogden Valley — big elevation swings, forced carries, and postcard Wasatch views. Beautiful but a serious walk; most players ride here.
